turns.user_message: I have finally realized that I am completely running on empty. This heavy feeling only started creeping in over the last few days, but it already feels like a wall I cannot climb. I wake up dreading the routine instead of just tackling it like I normally would. Every single task feels twice as heavy as it should, and my usual drive just vanished overnight. I keep waiting for it to pass, but right now I am just dragging my feet through everything.

I keep telling myself this recent slump will just fade away, but the exhaustion is sticking around much longer than I expected. What began as a rough patch has turned into a daily grind where my brain refuses to switch into gear. I sit down to focus and immediately feel a thick fog roll over my thoughts. My usual methods for pushing through the slump are completely failing me now. I am just watching my own momentum drain away while I struggle to keep up with the bare minimum.

I have officially hit a wall where my own mind just shuts down before I can even begin anything. This sudden emptiness that started recently has completely taken over my entire routine now. I stare at my screen for hours while my thoughts bounce around uselessly, unable to latch onto a single objective. Every notification feels like a physical weight pressing against my chest, and I just want to hide until the pressure lifts. I am completely out of fuel, and no amount of forcing myself is going to spark anything back to life.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This just started recently.
